title: Decision: Namespace chalk.json under a protocol key
description: Keep chalk.json top-level canonical (chalk.schema.json) so the Chalk Browser preserves our config on enrich

title: Lesson learned
description: When adding a read-only list/log command (decisions, lesson list, log), add a test for the empty/just-initialized state, not only the happy path where an entry already exists.

title: Lesson learned
description: Guard every readFileSync of a .chalk/* file with an existsSync check (or reuse the Store helper) so a missing file yields a friendly message instead of an unhandled throw.

description: When a command overloads the same positional argument as both a subcommand keyword and a free-text payload (e.g. `chalk lesson list`), provide an explicit subcommand or flag for the payload so it can

description: When making pipeline stages idempotent/resumable, add a resume test for each interrupt boundary (after branch, after commit, after pr-open), not just a single interrupt point, since each stage's guard

title: Loop: 1 merged, 0 blocked over 1 round(s)
Audit green (held-out regression)
Decision: Lever 3 (break-it gate) ships opt-in via protocol.breakTest
description: running ONE test file is language-specific; like e2e/regression it stays off until a per-file command template is set, so it can't false-block existing projects

title: Audit green (held-out regression)
Decision: Handoff docs live under .chalk/handoffs (gitignored, single-canonical via store.root)
description: ephemeral session-pickup artifacts like runs/; accessed through the Store so a worktree resolves them from the main checkout, no copy-in needed

title: Decision: Every block (manual, run-loop, pipeline) writes a handoff via the single chalk block / blockTask chokepoint
description: the pipeline auto-blocks by shelling out to chalk block, so wiring handoff there covers all three block paths without duplication; churn budget accumulates across work/unblock cycles, not within one run loop

title: Decision: chalk next --json is the one-session-per-task signal (freshSession + handoff path); buildContext folds the handoff in so a fresh session resumes
description: chalk stays a referee, not a session manager — an orchestrator reads the signal and seeds a clean session; the executor is already one fresh process per task

title: Audit green (held-out regression)
Decision: Amended acceptance test for "feat: rich 'what was done' PR body recording"
description: add the BYO-narrative failure-fallback assertion flagged in review (parallels handoff)

title: Decision: PR body is the canonical 'what was done' record; task.pr.recorded gates merge
description: humans review on GitHub, so the recording must live in the PR body, not just the spine; recorded flag lets the merge gate enforce a non-empty change set was documented

title: Decision: Remote review surfaced as PR COMMENTS + an LGTM marker, not a formal gh approval
description: GitHub forbids approving your own PR from the opening account; a comment works with the single account the pipeline uses; task.pr.lgtm carries the merge-gate signal

title: Decision: Amended acceptance test for "feat: merge gate requires recording + LGTM + broke-check, then merges"
description: add ciStatus garbage-payload assertion (med finding) to the locked test

title: Decision: Merge gate = brokeCheck (CI or local) ∧ recording ∧ (review-required → passing review + LGTM); merge posts LGTM if missing
description: centralizes the 'safe + accountable' contract in pure mergeBlockers; CI-or-local keeps it working with or without remote CI; merge guarantees an LGTM precedes the merge

title: Decision: Amended acceptance test for "feat: fix-reverify-rereview loop with churn budget and handoff"
description: reviewer caught: loop must PUSH the fix (else merge takes the stale branch); assert push each round + rounds on work-fail

title: Decision: The review fix-loop pushes the fix to the remote branch each round
description: merge squash-merges the REMOTE branch; without pushing the loop's fix, merge would silently take the stale rejected code (caught in adversarial review). pr-stage can't be re-run (it would re-create the PR), so the loop pushes directly

title: Decision: Planner surfaces scoping questions via a tolerant text convention (## Questions / Q: lines), not a JSON contract
description: planners are BYO claude -p; parsing keeps them flexible while still capturing what to validate; questions land in questions.json tied to the task

title: Decision: Plan-approval is an opt-in hard gate (protocol.plan.required): work refuses / the run pauses until chalk approve-plan
description: makes planning the human checkpoint the vision calls for — humans read the plan, answer scoping questions, approve; only then does chalk run end-to-end. Off by default so existing flows are unaffected

title: Audit green (held-out regression)
Decision: Release stage ships from the spine's done tasks (offline), marking each task released for idempotency
description: the dev cycle already records what was done; release groups done-but-unreleased tasks into notes + a semver bump from change types, tags, and marks them so re-runs are safe — no GitHub round-trip needed

title: Audit green (held-out regression)
Audit green (held-out regression)
Audit green (held-out regression)
Decision: Feedback loop = retro's engine fed by external product signals (.chalk/feedback/) instead of an internal run digest
description: closes the cycle ship→learn→backlog; reuses the proven {issues}-JSON + dedup + severity-floor filing pattern so signals become improvement issues the dev cycle then fixes

title: Decision: Feedback issues are filed via gh (like retro) and signals archived after processing for idempotency
description: reuses the issue→pull→task loop so product feedback enters the same gated dev cycle; archiving prevents re-analysis and re-filing

title: Audit green (held-out regression)
Audit green (held-out regression)
Audit green (held-out regression)
Decision: Discovery creates chalk TASKS with acceptance criteria directly (not GitHub issues like feedback)
description: discovery defines the contract (criteria) up front, so it produces backlog tasks the dev cycle works against; the plan-approval gate then lets a human validate the generated scope before code

title: Decision: Discovery resolves task deps by title (best-effort) and gates the generated backlog behind plan-approval
description: the agent emits human-readable after-titles; chalk resolves them to ids so the backlog has real ordering; plan.required means a human validates the proposed scope before work

title: Audit green (held-out regression)
Audit green (held-out regression)
Audit green (held-out regression)
Decision: Amended acceptance test for "feat: portal model — map the chalk spine to the portal schema"
description: client-privacy: drop non-client-safe events (don't relabel) so internal titles can't leak to the portal

title: Decision: Portal data is a deterministic transform of the chalk spine (not a codebase scrape), and drops non-client-safe events
description: chalk's structured spine (tasks/milestones/updates) maps exactly to the portal schema, more precise than the extract-portal-data skill's scrape; dropping internal event types protects the client view

title: Decision: Portal files are written as JSON (valid YAML) for robustness/zero-dep instead of a hand-rolled YAML emitter
description: JSON is a strict subset of YAML so any portal YAML reader parses it identically; avoids subtle indentation/quoting bugs in a hand-written serializer with no yaml dependency

title: Audit green (held-out regression)
Audit green (held-out regression)
Decision: Released v0.1.0
description: 30 change(s); tagged v0.1.0

title: Released v0.1.0 (30 change(s))
Decision: Live testing found two bugs the per-command suites missed: release ignored --dry-run, portal mis-resolved an absolute --out (join vs resolve)
description: exercising the real commands end-to-end exposed gaps stub-based unit tests didn't; added --dry-run to release and switched portal to resolve()

title: Audit green (held-out regression)
Audit green (held-out regression)
Decision: Cross-model adversarial review: chalk doctor warns when the P5 reviewer shares the executor's model
description: self-preference bias (arxiv 2410.21819) + correlated reviewer/generator failure (2604.08401): a same-model adversary self-prefers and shares blind spots. Chalk can't pick the model (BYO executor) so it surfaces the risk via doctor + a cross-family recommendation. M3 of the harness-improvement plan.

title: Decision: Harden P7 blindness: doctor fails on git-tracked held-out; audit output-withholding pinned
description: ImpossibleBench — isolating tests drops cheating to ~0, leaking them restores it. A worktree is a plain checkout, so a committed held-out file lands in the agent's sandbox. doctor now refuses it. Reviewer noted the withholding test pins the console-leak (inherit) regression but not pipe-without-print (not itself a leak). M1a+M1c.

title: Decision: Amended acceptance test for "feat: mutation-testing adequacy gate — block a change whose changed code has surviving mutants (lever 3, rigorous)"
description: reviewer fix→re-review: (med) assert the implementation-file filter excludes tests + .chalk; (low) assert {file} substitution; (low) a tool that can't run is inconclusive, not a false survivor

title: Decision: Mutation-testing adequacy gate (rigorous lever 3): lib/mutation.mjs, opt-in via protocol.mutation, wired into work + run driver
description: passing tests != adequate — a benchmark test hit 100% coverage / 4% mutation; surviving mutants in changed code = weak assertions (Meta runs this in prod; Stryker --incremental/cargo-mutants --in-diff make it per-change feasible). Adversarial review caught an unasserted impl-file filter; fixed via amend-spec. Generalizes break-it. M2.

title: Decision: M5: amend-spec invalidates a prior passing review (marks it 'stale'); review re-runs when the last verdict no longer stands
description: closes the bypass 'get a pass → weaken the locked test via amend-spec → merge on the stale approval'. done/merge P5 checks already require last verdict 'pass'; the fix makes a changed locked test drop that. force-review --why requirement pinned by test. Live-caught: manual 'chalk review' dies on a transient parseVerdict greedy-regex failure (no retry like the pipeline) — robustness follow-up (C1).

title: Decision: M4: size-scaled P7 stringency — heldOutFloor(loc, locPerTest) makes the held-out count a floor that grows with code size; audit warns, phase gate refuses (overridable)
description: SpecBench: the held-out-vs-visible gap grows ~28pts per 10x LOC, so a fixed oracle decays. Previously codeSize only triggered staleness; now the bar actually rises with the code — making 'stringency scales with code size' a real mechanism instead of a doc claim. Default locPerTest 2000 keeps tiny projects at floor 0. M4.

title: Decision: C1: robust reviewer verdict parsing — lib/json.mjs balanced-brace scanner (jsonObjects/parseLastJson); review.mjs parseVerdict recovers the last valid verdict object
description: the greedy /{...}/ span grabbed from a stray brace in the reviewer's prose to EOF and failed to parse, blocking a real adversarial review twice this session. Now scans for balanced top-level objects (ignoring braces inside strings) and recovers the operative one. The same fragile pattern remains in retro/feedback/discovery — C2 de-drift follow-up (adopt parseLastJson there).

title: Decision: C2: retro/feedback/discovery adopt the robust parseLastJson — duplicated greedy /{...}/ JSON regex removed
description: the same fragile parse that broke the C1 reviewer lived in all three lifecycle agents; they now recover their {lessons,issues}/{issues}/{tasks} payload from prose-wrapped output via lib/json.mjs (shape-predicate per module). Reviewer noted an unbalanced lone-brace corner case — outside contract, and the old regex failed on it too (not a regression).

title: Decision: Amended acceptance test for "fix: chalk review retries once on a transient reviewer failure (match the pipeline stage)"
description: reviewer (test-adequacy): cover the fatal error→error path and that the retry is BOUNDED (invocation-count assertion); add a --no-retry suppression test

title: Decision: C1-remainder: chalk review retries once on a transient reviewer failure; pipeline + reviewloop pass --no-retry (they own stage-level retry)
description: a manual/direct 'chalk review' died on the first transient flake (a truncated response, then a connection drop) twice this session, unlike the pipeline stage. Now a bounded retry-once — only a second consecutive error is fatal. Reviewer's test-adequacy note addressed via amend-spec, which invalidated the prior pass (M5) and forced a re-review.

title: Decision: Release hardening: chalk release tags FIRST; in a git repo a tag failure is fatal before writing CHANGELOG or marking tasks released
description: it used to swallow a failed git tag yet mark work released, shipping onto an untagged version that the next release (seeing them marked) would never re-tag. A non-git project legitimately can't tag → stays a CHANGELOG/pkg-only release. First of the silent-failure autonomous-path hardenings.
